articles.title: Rajasthan records unusual rise in temperatures: Day and night warmer than normal; more heat expected ahead - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: ("mercury soars" OR "mercury crosses" OR "temperature soars" OR "record temperature" OR "all-time high" OR "degrees Celsius" OR "maximum temperature" OR "hottest day") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan records unusual rise in temperatures:Day and night warmer than normal; more heat expected ahead
- Copy link
Heat is increasing in Rajasthan. On Tuesday, the maximum temperature in many cities of the state was recorded above 35 degrees Celsius. In the western districts, the heat was scorching during the day.
In Barmer, the maximum temperature crossed 38 degrees Celsius. Due to a slight drop in the minimum temperature, some cities experienced a slight coolness on Tuesday night, but the temperature remained above normal.
The Meteorological Center Jaipur has predicted an increase in temperature in the state in the next 2-3 days, with the heat intensifying during the day.
In the last 24 hours, the sky remained completely clear and strong sunlight appeared from the morning. In Pilani, Chittorgarh, Pali, Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, Phalodi, Bikaner, Churu, Nagaur, Jalore and Lunkaransar, the maximum temperature on Tuesday was recorded at 35 degrees Celsius or above.
Temperature in all cities above 31 degrees
On Tuesday, the maximum temperature in all cities of Rajasthan was recorded above 31 degrees Celsius. The maximum temperature recorded was 31.4 in Alwar, 33.9 in Jaipur and Udaipur, 32.5 in Sikar, 33.3 in Ganganagar, 32.9 in Baran, 31.2 in Hanumangarh, 32.8 in Sirohi, and 33.5 degrees Celsius in Jhunjhunu.
Major Cities of the State – Temperature (Temperature as on 03 March, in degrees Celsius)
Due to the intense heat, fans have now started running in homes and offices in many cities during mornings and evenings. Whereas in the western districts, coolers and ACs have started operating during the day.
Night temperature dropped, coolness increased
Although the daytime temperature in the state is rising, the minimum temperature on Tuesday night saw a drop of 1 to 2 degrees in many cities.
The minimum temperature was recorded below 15 degrees Celsius in Churu, Sikar, Alwar, Tonk, Karauli, Dausa, Jhunjhunu, and Baran. Yesterday, the lowest temperature was recorded at 10.5 degrees Celsius in Fatehpur. This drop in temperature resulted in a slight coolness in some cities during the night.
